source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings
source.population: Adults, children and young people at risk of or involved in violence or aggression, with age- and setting-specific recommendations.
source.setting: Mental health, general health, community and emergency-department settings, including post-incident review.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected objective risk-context, de-escalation, restrictive-intervention monitoring and post-incident documentation. It does not authorise restraint, tranquillisation, seclusion or any medicine and requires UAE legal and facility adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.14 and 1.3.12–1.3.20 — risk assessment and de-escalation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports objective multidisciplinary risk assessment, prior incident and trigger context, service-user involvement, previously effective de-escalation and transfer of an existing risk assessment.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.exclusions: Exact for selected objective risk-context, de-escalation, restrictive-intervention monitoring and post-incident documentation. It does not authorise restraint, tranquillisation, seclusion or any medicine and requires UAE legal and facility adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.8–1.2.14 and 1.3.12–1.3.20 — risk assessment and de-escalation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports objective multidisciplinary risk assessment, prior incident and trigger context, service-user involvement, previously effective de-escalation and transfer of an existing risk assessment.

items.rationale: The exact NICE risk and de-escalation section supports recording associated context, relevant negatives, patient concerns and an objective clinician risk context, including triggers and previously effective de-escalation, without asserting dangerousness or selecting an intervention.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting airway, breathing, circulation, vital signs, physical and psychological monitoring and clinician review only when a restrictive intervention actually occurred. Medicine instructions and doses are excluded.

source.title: Violence and aggression: short-term management in mental health, health and community settings
source.population: Adults, children and young people at risk of or involved in violence or aggression, with age- and setting-specific recommendations.
source.setting: Mental health, general health, community and emergency-department settings, including post-incident review.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected objective risk-context, de-escalation, restrictive-intervention monitoring and post-incident documentation. It does not authorise restraint, tranquillisation, seclusion or any medicine and requires UAE legal and facility adaptation.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting airway, breathing, circulation, vital signs, physical and psychological monitoring and clinician review only when a restrictive intervention actually occurred. Medicine instructions and doses are excluded.

items.rationale: The exact safety-monitoring section supports measured vital signs, manual focused examination, severity, appearance and time-critical abnormalities only if an intervention or injury was actually assessed; medicine and restraint instructions are excluded.

items.final_wording: Supports recording harm, ongoing risk, emotional impact, contributing factors, patient and witness perspectives, completed documentation, handover and formal review after an incident.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.4.53–1.4.63 — post-incident debrief, documentation and review

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording harm, ongoing risk, emotional impact, contributing factors, patient and witness perspectives, completed documentation, handover and formal review after an incident.

items.rationale: The exact post-incident section supports recording clinician-decided disposition, reviewed reports, documentation plan, completed discussion and arranged handover or review after an incident; it does not authorise an intervention.
